summary: A personal accident insurance policy offering coverage up to Rs. 1 Crore with multiple sum insured options (30L, 50L, 1Cr). The policy covers accidental death, permanent total disablement, permanent partial disablement, temporary total disablement, and various other accident-related benefits including hospitalization, fractures, burns, and child education support.
coverage: Permanent Total Disablement (PTD) - Up to PTD SI as per PTD Table
Permanent Total Disablement (PTD) Improvement - Additional amount up to PTD SI as per PTD Table
Permanent Partial Disablement (PPD) - Up to PPD SI as per PPD Table
Permanent Partial Disablement (PPD) Improvement - Additional amount up to PPD SI as per PPD Table
Temporary Total Disablement Plus - 1% of SI per week, maximum 100 weeks, maximum up to 50 lac
Fractures - Up to Rs. 1lac/2lac/3lac as per Fracture Table based on SI
Major Diagnostic Tests - Up to Rs. 15,000/25,000/50,000 based on SI
Mobility Cover - Up to Rs. 15,000/25,000/50,000 based on SI
Burns - Up to Rs. 10,00,000/20,00,000/25,00,000 as per Table based on SI
Domestic Road Ambulance - Up to Rs. 5,000 (for 50L and 1Cr plans)
Nursing Care - Rs. 1000/1500 per day, Max 15 days per Claim (for 50L and 1Cr plans)
Reconstructive Surgery - Up to Rs. 10,00,000/20,00,000 (for 50L and 1Cr plans)
Repatriation of Mortal Remains - Lower of 2% of the SI or Rs 1L (for 50L and 1Cr plans)
Accidental Hospitalization - Up to 3lac/5L/5lac based on SI
Loyalty Benefit - 5% of the SI every renewal; Max. up to 50% of SI
claims_information: Weekly Compensation amount settled will be 1% of SI for Temporary Total Disablement
Customer will have to submit proof of Temporary Total disablement due to Accident
Weekly compensation is payable for Max 100 weeks
Total amount paid under Temporary Total Disablement Plus will not exceed 50L
Original Bills need to be submitted for Major Diagnostic Tests reimbursement
Diagnostic tests to be undertaken on the written advice of a Medical Practitioner
Diagnostic tests are conducted within 3 months from the date of Accident
Qualified Nurse should be hired within 1 week of discharge from hospital for Nursing Care
Reconstructive surgery has to be carried out within 30 days from the date of Accident
Guardians have to submit a valid document establishing the Age of child and relationship between the child and the customer at the time of claim
exclusions: People working in Mines & Oil and Gas Industry
Crew Employed In Ships (Merchant Navy when in Duty)
Labourers working in the Construction Industry or Daily wagers
Professional Sports Persons or Professional Drivers
People Associated with Racing & Adventure Sports
Armed forces (Army, Navy, Air force including armed guards) or People working with Explosives and Firecrackers
Glass workers including Workers working in Glass Furnaces or Furnace Operators
People working Underground or at heights

faqs.question: What are the sum insured options available?
faqs.answer: The policy offers three sum insured options: Rs. 30 Lakhs, Rs. 50 Lakhs, and Rs. 1 Crore.

faqs.question: What is the minimum and maximum entry age?
faqs.answer: Minimum entry age is 18 years and maximum entry age is 64 years.

faqs.question: What is the maximum renewal age?
faqs.answer: The policy can be renewed lifelong with no maximum renewal age limit.

faqs.question: What policy tenure options are available?
faqs.answer: Policy can be taken for 1, 2, or 3 years.

faqs.question: What is covered under Accidental Death benefit?
faqs.answer: Accidental Death benefit provides 100% of Sum Insured in case of death due to accident.

faqs.question: How much compensation is provided for Temporary Total Disablement?
faqs.answer: 1% of Sum Insured per week is provided, for maximum 100 weeks, with total amount not exceeding Rs. 50 lakhs.

faqs.question: What is the Child Education benefit amount?
faqs.answer: Child Education benefit provides 10% of Sum Insured for all sum insured options.

faqs.question: What conditions apply for Child Education benefit?
faqs.answer: The child must be financially dependent on the customer, have no independent sources of income, and not have attained 25 years of age.

faqs.question: What is the Major Diagnostic Tests coverage?
faqs.answer: Coverage is up to Rs. 15,000 for 30L plan, Rs. 25,000 for 50L plan, and Rs. 50,000 for 1Cr plan.

faqs.question: What diagnostic tests are covered?
faqs.answer: Major diagnostic tests like CT Scan, MRI etc. are covered when undertaken on written advice of a Medical Practitioner.

faqs.question: What is the time limit for diagnostic tests after accident?
faqs.answer: Diagnostic tests must be conducted within 3 months from the date of accident.

faqs.question: What is covered under Disappearance benefit?
faqs.answer: Disappearance benefit provides 100% of Sum Insured for all sum insured options.

faqs.question: What is the Burns coverage amount?
faqs.answer: Burns coverage is up to Rs. 10,00,000 for 30L plan, Rs. 20,00,000 for 50L plan, and Rs. 25,00,000 for 1Cr plan.

faqs.question: Is Domestic Road Ambulance covered in all plans?
faqs.answer: No, Domestic Road Ambulance coverage of up to Rs. 5,000 is available only in 50L and 1Cr plans.

faqs.question: What is the Nursing Care benefit?
faqs.answer: Rs. 1000 per day for 50L plan and Rs. 1500 per day for 1Cr plan, maximum 15 days per claim.

faqs.question: When should a Qualified Nurse be hired for Nursing Care benefit?
faqs.answer: Qualified Nurse should be hired within 1 week of discharge from hospital.

faqs.question: What is the time limit for Reconstructive Surgery?
faqs.answer: Reconstructive surgery has to be carried out within 30 days from the date of accident.

faqs.question: What is covered under Repatriation of Mortal Remains?
faqs.answer: Transportation expenses of customer's body from place of death to city of last known address, limited to lower of 2% of SI or Rs. 1 lakh.

faqs.question: How does the Loyalty Benefit work?
faqs.answer: Coverage amount of AD & PTD is enhanced by flat 5% of Sum Insured on cumulative basis as Loyalty Bonus on renewal, maximum up to 50% of SI.

faqs.question: What happens to Loyalty Bonus if policy is not renewed?
faqs.answer: The entire Loyalty Bonus will be forfeited if the Policy is not continued/renewed.

faqs.question: What is the premium for 30L sum insured?
faqs.answer: The premium is Rs. 7,575 (inclusive of 18% GST) for ages 18-64 years.

faqs.question: What is the premium for 50L sum insured?
faqs.answer: The premium is Rs. 13,625 (inclusive of 18% GST) for ages 18-64 years.

faqs.question: What is the premium for 1Cr sum insured?
faqs.answer: The premium is Rs. 25,451 (inclusive of 18% GST) for ages 18-64 years.

faqs.question: What income multiplier applies for salaried persons?
faqs.answer: For salaried persons, the income multiplier is 20 times Gross Income.

faqs.question: What income multiplier applies for business income persons?
faqs.answer: For persons with business income, the income multiplier is 25 times Net Income.

faqs.question: What income multiplier applies for pensioners?
faqs.answer: For persons with passive income like pensioners, the income multiplier is 10 times Net Income.

faqs.question: What financial documents are required for sum insured above Rs. 1 crore?
faqs.answer: Last 3 years ITR filed including computation of Income, Business P&L audited statement (for business), and Net-worth Statement.

faqs.question: What percentage is paid for total loss of sight of both eyes under PTD?
faqs.answer: 100% of Sum Insured is paid for total and irrecoverable loss of sight of both eyes.

faqs.question: What percentage is paid for loss of one eye under PTD?
faqs.answer: 50% of Sum Insured is paid for total and irrecoverable loss of sight of one eye.

faqs.question: What percentage is paid for loss of hearing in both ears under PPD?
faqs.answer: 75% of Sum Insured is paid for total and irrecoverable loss of hearing in both ears.

faqs.question: What percentage is paid for third degree burns of 30% or more body surface?
faqs.answer: 100% of the Burns coverage amount is paid for third degree burns of 30% or more of total body surface area.

faqs.question: What is the maximum payout for Hip or Pelvis compound fractures?
faqs.answer: 100% of the Fractures Sum Insured is paid for Hip or Pelvis multiple fractures with at least one Compound Fracture and one Complete Fracture.

faqs.question: Can the policy cover family members?
faqs.answer: The policy is individual cover type, and the proposer has to be insured (Self coverage only).
